findings.title: 409 test mock is a plain object, not a real ApiError instance
findings.why_it_matters: The test rejects with `{ name: 'ApiError', status: 409, message: '...' }`, a plain object literal, rather than `new ApiError(...)` from '../../api/client'. Every other ApiError-rejection test in this codebase (Settings/*.spec.ts, SitesPage.spec.ts, BatchCampaignPage.spec.ts, ChannelBindingCard.spec.ts) constructs a real `new ApiError(message, status, payload)` instance, and the established idiom for status-specific handling elsewhere in this codebase is `e instanceof ApiError && e.status === 422` (BatchCampaignPage.vue, SitesPage.vue, Settings/*.vue). The mock also omits `payload` entirely, even though the same file's own comment above bulkPublishDraftsNow documents that the 409 response carries `payload.error_class === 'already_running'`. Today this doesn't mask a bug because run()/reportError never branches on `instanceof ApiError` or reads `.payload` for this path -- it always falls through to the generic classifyError('unknown') toast. But if a future change adds a distinguishing 409 branch (following the exact `instanceof ApiError` pattern already used for 422 elsewhere in this repo), this test would keep passing against a mock that could never satisfy that check, silently failing to catch the regression it's nominally testing.
findings.suggested_fix: Import ApiError from '../../api/client' and reject with `new ApiError('Bulk publish already in progress', 409, { error_class: 'already_running' })` to match the shape the comment in drafts.ts documents and the construction pattern used by every other ApiError test in the repo.
evidence: frontend/src/pages/Drafts/DraftsPage.spec.ts:177-179 rejects with a plain object literal: `{ name: 'ApiError', status: 409, message: 'Bulk publish already in progress' }`
frontend/src/api/drafts.ts:46-49 comment: 'rejects with ApiError (status 409, payload.error_class === "already_running")' -- payload is not present in the test mock
frontend/src/api/client.ts:43-51 defines ApiError as a class extending Error with status/payload fields, not matched by the plain-object mock

Repo convention: frontend/src/pages/Sites/SitesPage.spec.ts:89, frontend/src/pages/BatchCampaign/BatchCampaignPage.spec.ts:87, frontend/src/pages/Settings/*.spec.ts all construct `new ApiError(...)` for rejection mocks

Repo convention: frontend/src/pages/BatchCampaign/BatchCampaignPage.vue:78, frontend/src/pages/Sites/SitesPage.vue:82, frontend/src/pages/Settings/*.vue:~64-128 all branch on `e instanceof ApiError && e.status === ...` -- the exact check this mock would fail

residual_risks: classifyError (frontend/src/lib/errors.ts) maps HTTP 409 into the generic 'unknown' category, identical to any unrecognized status -- the same fixed '出错了' toast the user would see for an unrelated 500 or malformed response. This matches the plan's stated intent (busy-state disables the button; 409 must not fake a success toast) but means the backend's specific 'already_running' signal is discarded client-side with no differentiated messaging, by design rather than oversight in this diff.

DraftsPage's bulk-cancel button (`onBulkCancel` -> bulkCancelDrafts) is enabled whenever `selected.size > 0`, with no client-side check that every selected id is actually in 'scheduled' status (the per-row cancel button, by contrast, only renders for scheduled rows). A user could select a mix of pending and scheduled drafts and trigger bulk-cancel; how the backend handles non-scheduled ids in that call is not visible from the frontend diff alone.
testing_gaps: The 409/error-path test exists only for bulkPublishDraftsNow in DraftsPage.spec.ts; there is no equivalent rejection test for bulkCancelDrafts, and HistoryPage.spec.ts has no rejection/error-path test at all for the new bulkRecheckHistory.

No test exercises bulkPublishDraftsNow/bulkCancelDrafts/bulkRecheckHistory being called with a mixed-status selection (e.g., a pending + scheduled draft selected together for bulk-cancel), so the UI's assumption that any non-empty selection is valid for every bulk action is untested.
